key_insight: DFT can miss large local stacking-energy differences in bilayer InSe, because the energetics depend on the full registry and many-body electronic response, not just the interfacial Se motif.
engineering_relevance: Moiré models built from DFT may significantly underestimate corrugation, affecting predictions of structural relaxation, domain formation, and electronic reconstruction in twisted layered materials.
method: Benchmarked bilayer InSe stacking energetics with diffusion quantum Monte Carlo (DMC).
Compared DMC against DFT for AB, AAr, and ABr registries with the same interfacial Se arrangement.
Analyzed local-registry charge-density response and energy separations across stackings.
evidence_signal: DMC split stackings that DFT found nearly degenerate: AB/AAr/ABr differed by 8(5) and 41(4) meV/f.u., and the full registry range reached 60(7) meV/f.u.
watch_item: The benchmark is specific to bilayer InSe, so transferring the conclusion to other moiré materials still requires system-specific validation.
paper_title: Many-body benchmarking of DFT local-registry energetics in bilayer InSe
